edibility: The world's most expensive culinary fungus; eaten raw, shaved over warm dishes; never cooked (heat destroys aroma).
taste: Pungent garlicky-cheesy aroma; intense umami; almost overwhelming when fresh.
search: Where/when to find — Oct-Dec in Piedmont (Italy) and parts of Croatia/Slovenia, calcareous soil with oak/hazel/poplar; only trained dogs locate; cannot be cultivated.
